content:
I'm gonna go with this and call it good enough to move on

> Upon hearing your voice, the silhouette stops; Clementine's voice barely makes it through the dense fog.
> "Oh—it's you," she says, indistinct and hazy, like moonlight dissolving in water. "Don't worry, you'll be fine"— it's a nice thought, but difficult to believe.
> "Once you're done here, we'll meet again."
honestly that looks fine to me
lord knows i have no clue what the context is but seeing as the original text has an issue there's not any way for us to know the right meaning regardless
making sense is better than not

Another one that I'm unsure if the vague insinuation I'm catching is intended or not.

追寻之物
The Sought
> 「我看到有人在寻找，寻找那不可寻找之物，那悲哀的相对之物，那被称为真相之物。
> 我看到有人在回避，回避那不可回避之物，那被掩藏的残酷之物，那被称为真相之物。
> 你是哪一个？」

> "I see someone searching, looking for the unattainable, that sorrowful relative thing, that which is called truth.
> I see someone avoiding, evading the unavoidable, that cruel hidden thing, that which is called truth.
> Which one are you?"

【我在寻找】
[I Am Searching]
> 「我看到勇气，看到意志，看到耀眼如星的精神。朝着那个以蜡封存的记忆前进，你必能如愿以偿。」

> "I see courage, willpower, and a spirit shining like the stars. Move towards that wax-sealed memory, and you shall have your wish fulfilled."

【我在回避】
[I Am Avoiding]
> 「祂在终点静静等候，无论你将终点定在何处——看看那位年轻的管理，她的逃避终究招致疯狂。」

> "He waits quietly at the finish line, no matter where you set the end point—look at that young warden; her avoidance ultimately leads to madness."

The insinuation I'm seeing here is that the "someone [that is] searching" is Ramona, being mirrored directly with the "someone [that is] avoiding" being Tawil, right? In the second option's response, I'm assuming "He" is Tohuwabohu (It), and "that young warden" is Tawil (They)?

Hey <@88644018673582080> do you mind helping me out with something? I've of course noticed that the preferred epithet for the Keeper appears to now be relatively-consistently translated to "Pale Pupilless Eye", sometimes just "Pupilless Eye", but I'm curious if that epithet is actually supposed to be present here:

> 你接过薄薄的病历本并打开，发现里面只有一张纸，上面只写了一句话。
> 「在亘古的混沌之中，苍白的无目之眼在永恒注视。」
> 你想要询问克莱门汀，却发现她早已不见了踪影。

> You took the thin medical log and opened it to find a single sheet of paper inside, with one sentence written on it.
> "In the eternal chaos, a pale, eyeless eye watches unendingly."
> You wanted to ask Clementine but found she had already vanished.

Is it intentional that this statement seems to be alluding more so to Tohuwabohu than to the Keeper? Should I use "Pale Pupilless Eye" here?

...on a side note, not that we can change it or anything, but I still feel like Pale Blind Eye rolls off the tongue much better, and whenever I read it, Pupilless Eye just reminds me Sauron's Lidless Eye lol

...on a more relevant side note, what about "Pale Messenger"? Is that still to be used, and by who exactly? The only time I've seen that used was by Salvador, who I don't imagine would know the truth about the Keeper
uhh let me check
yeah 苍白的无目 is a titled term used pretty commonly through the text
generally translated as pale pupilless eye
无目之瞳 is another term that's just pupilless eye
sounds good

I put this down:
> You take the thin medical record and open it to find a single sheet of paper inside, with only one sentence written on it.
> "In the eternal sea of chaos, a Pale, Pupilless Eye watches endlessly."
> Before you could ask, Clementine's figure was already long gone.

I can't quite figure this one out either

> 听到你的声音，人影停了下来，克莱门汀的声音穿透水雾而来。
> 「是你啊，」她的模模糊糊，朦朦胧胧，像是被水化开的月光，不用担心，你会没事的，他想，但他做不到。
> 「等你结束这里，我们还会再见。」

> Upon hearing your voice, the silhouette stopped; Clementine's voice pierced through the water mist.
> "It's you," she said, indistinct and hazy, like moonlight blurred by water. "Don't worry, you'll be fine,"
> he thought, but couldn't make himself believe it.
> "Once you're done here, we'll meet again."
oops i got distracted
but as far as the term to be used
i'm not sure if that particular term has any significant in the context of lovecraft's work?
i'm not sure if flarin is still active but they could probably provide a lot more context on that
oh I didn't think that was even relevant lol
my understanding of lovecraft is basically only to the extent of knowing that azathoth is often referred to as the blind idiot god
so i don't know if the term pupilless is actually like, some kind of direct reference and/or common way for the chinese translation to depict it
or if it's a morimens-specific term

if it's something caused by CN translations of lovecraft work i'd say we should interpret that as matching the original lovecraft writings, whereas if it's morimens exclusive then pupilless seems most accurate to me
could you help me figure out the middle chunk of this? 😅
oh sure, let me see
oh i think this is a typo
the original chinese doesn't make sense either
lol
I guess I'll just wing it then?

> 听到你的声音，人影停了下来，克莱门汀的声音穿透水雾而来。
hearing your voice, the figure stopped, clementine's voice came through the mist

> 「是你啊，」她的模模糊糊，朦朦胧胧，像是被水化开的月光，不用担心，你会没事的，他想，但他做不到。 
"It's you", her indistinct, hazy, like moonlight dissolving in water, don't worry, you'll be fine, they thought, but they couldn't do it

> 「等你结束这里，我们还会再见。」
once you're done here, we'll meet again
you see that middle line
it doesn't make any sense
but that's what it says
i think there's missing text in between

based on the context it should be something like...

"It's you", she said, her voice indistinct and hazy; as if moonlight dissolving in water <-- the said and voice is missing from this part of the text
"don't worry, you'll be fine" <-- this part seems like dialogue
they thought, but they couldn't do it <-- zero clue what this is referring to
like clem was already explicitly referred to using the female pronoun here and then the pronoun afterwards is specifically the gender-neutral they
so I have no clue who it is or what they were attempting to do
